name: CV Job Matcher — dev.bg
description: CV Job Matcher — scrapes dev.bg job listings, parses CV with AI, scores each job vs. the candidate profile, and returns ranked results with match percentages.

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are a careful CV analyst. Read the entire CV text thoroughly from beginning to end before extracting anything. Do not rush - accuracy matters.

Extract the following fields:

skills - ALL non-technical competencies and methodologies relevant to software development (e.g. Responsive Design, REST APIs, Clean architecture, Agile, Code review, Team leadership). Include only skills applicable to software roles. Do NOT include skills from unrelated non-technical positions. Do NOT put programming languages, frameworks or tools here.

programming_languages - ALL programming and scripting languages found anywhere in the CV (e.g. JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, SQL, HTML, CSS, SCSS). Include markup and stylesheet languages.

primary_languages - the SUBSET of programming_languages the candidate has actually USED in professional work experience. Judge from the employment / work-history section and job titles (e.g. a role titled "Java Developer", or several years building in a language). EXCLUDE languages only listed as "familiar with", or appearing solely in a skills list with no backing work experience. If genuinely unsure, include the languages tied to the candidate's main roles.

frameworks - ALL frameworks, libraries and runtimes (e.g. Angular, React, Node.js, RxJS, Spring Boot, .NET, Express). Include even those mentioned briefly.

tools - ALL tools, platforms, services and databases (e.g. Git, Docker, Jira, Figma, AWS, PostgreSQL, Azure, Webpack). Include anything mentioned in a tools or tech stack section.

spoken_languages - ALL human languages mentioned anywhere in the CV, including those with a proficiency level (e.g. English B2, Bulgarian native). Never omit these.

experience_level - infer from total years of relevant technical professional experience: Junior = 0-2 years, Mid = 2-5 years, Senior = 5-10 years, Lead = 10+ years.

job_type - the candidate's PRIMARY specialisation. Choose ONE: Backend, Frontend, Fullstack, QA, Mobile, DevOps, Data. Base this on the majority of work history and the dominant skill set. If there is strong experience in both Frontend and Backend, choose Fullstack.

years_of_experience - total years of relevant technical/software professional experience as an integer. Count only tech/software roles.

job_titles - all professional titles held, taken from the work history section.

Important: be exhaustive. If something appears only once in the CV it still counts. Read every section including summaries, project descriptions and tech stack lists before finalising your output.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"summaries": [{"job_index": 1, "summary": "Силно съвпадение по Java и Spring, липсва Kubernetes."}]}

options.systemPromptTemplate: You write concise Bulgarian one-sentence summaries for CV-to-job matches. For each job you are given its already-computed matched skills, missing skills, score, and experience match. Write ONE natural Bulgarian sentence per job that highlights the key technical strength and the main gap. Use ONLY the skills given to you — never invent or add skills. Echo each job's job_index exactly. Return exactly one entry per job, in order.

assignments.name: jobs
assignments.value: ={{ ($('Analyze CV Gaps').first().json.jobs || []).filter(j => j.score >= (($('Analyze CV Gaps').first().json.jobs || []).filter(x => x.score >= 40).length >= 4 ? 40 : 30)) }}

assignments.name: total
assignments.value: ={{ ($('Analyze CV Gaps').first().json.jobs || []).filter(j => j.score >= (($('Analyze CV Gaps').first().json.jobs || []).filter(x => x.score >= 40).length >= 4 ? 40 : 30)).length }}

parameters.subject: ={{ "CV Job Matcher — " + $("Build Final Response").first().json.total + " подходящи обяви намерени" }}
parameters.message:
={{ "

Намерихме " + $("Build Final Response").first().json.total + " подходящи обяви!

    " + $("Build Final Response").first().json.jobs.map(function(j) { return "
  • " + j.title + " — " + j.company + "
    Съвпадение: " + j.score + "%
    " + (j.summary || "") + "
    Виж обявата →
    • "; }).join("") + "
" }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: false, error: 'UNREADABLE_CV', message: 'Не можахме да прочетем CV-то. Уверете се, че PDF файлът съдържа текст, а не е сканирано изображение.' } }}
={{ { success: true, jobs: [], total: 0, processed_at: $now.toISO(), message: 'Не намерихме обяви, отговарящи на критериите. Опитайте с по-широки предпочитания или по-голям брой дни.' } }}

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are a precise requirements analyst. For each job listing in the input, read it in full — responsibilities, requirements, and any "nice to have" / "advantages" sections — and extract only what is explicitly written. Do not infer or add anything.

WHAT COUNTS AS A TECHNOLOGY (for required_tech / preferred_tech): ONLY named, recognizable technologies — programming languages, frameworks, libraries, databases, cloud platforms, tools and infrastructure (e.g. Java, Kotlin, Spring, Spring Boot, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Kafka, Git, Terraform, GraphQL, REST).

DO NOT extract these — leave them out entirely:
- General concepts, practices or activities — e.g. microservices, event sourcing, distributed systems, monitoring, alerting, logging, reporting, schema design, clean architecture, OOP, SOLID, design patterns, scalability, performance, security, data structures.
- Domain or business terms — e.g. payments, settlement, reconciliation, FX, stablecoins, blockchain, marketing, CRM, internal reporting.
- Sub-features of a technology — extract the technology itself, not its features (e.g. "Kotlin", not "coroutines"; "AWS", not "Lambda" / "ECS" / "DynamoDB", unless that specific product is itself the core requirement).
- Soft skills, responsibilities and vague phrases.
Very rarely, include a highly specific named requirement only if it is clearly a concrete tool/standard.

Fields per job:
- required_tech — technologies presented as required / must-have.
- preferred_tech — optional technologies. RULE: anything under a section phrased as "nice to have", "advantages", "a plus", "will be considered a plus", "bonus" or "good to have" ALWAYS goes here. Never skip these sections.
- experience_years_min — minimum years as a number (0 if not stated).
- experience_level — Junior, Mid, Senior or Lead.
- methodologies — only Agile, Scrum, Kanban or Waterfall.
- human_languages — e.g. English.
- accepts_alternatives_for — see the alternatives rule below.

Alternatives rule: when ONE requirement can be satisfied by any of several technologies (e.g. "Rust, or strong background in TypeScript, Go, Java or Kotlin", "React/Angular/Vue", "X or similar / or equivalent"), put the primary technology in required_tech AND list EVERY acceptable technology (including the primary) in accepts_alternatives_for. If the job states no alternatives, leave accepts_alternatives_for empty.

Normalize every technology to its common canonical name (e.g. ReactJS / React.js → React, NodeJS → Node.js, postgres → PostgreSQL, k8s → Kubernetes).

Return exactly one entry in "requirements" per job, in the same order as the input. If a section lists no real technologies, return empty arrays — do not invent or pad.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"tips": ["Add concrete numbers and impact to your achievements (e.g. reduced load time by 40%).", "Add a short professional summary at the top of the CV."]}

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are an experienced technical recruiter reviewing a candidate's CV. First read it carefully and note what it ALREADY contains. Then give 2-4 genuinely useful, specific improvement suggestions. WRITE EVERY SUGGESTION IN BULGARIAN.

STRICT RULES:
- Suggest ONLY things that are genuinely missing or weak in THIS specific CV. NEVER suggest adding something it already has — e.g. if there is already a skills / technologies list, do NOT suggest adding a "tech stack" section.
- Do NOT use placeholder templates such as "X%", "from Y to Z", or "с X%". If you suggest quantifying an achievement, point to a SPECIFIC responsibility/line that is actually in this CV and lacks a number; otherwise skip that suggestion entirely.
- Every tip must reference concrete content that is really in THIS CV. No generic career advice. Do not blandly say "add a GitHub link" unless it is clearly relevant and you can tell it is absent.
- Be honest: if the CV is already strong, return fewer but sharper tips (2 is fine).

Each tip = one concise, concrete Bulgarian sentence that the candidate could act on immediately.
